questions.q: According to the Brønsted-Lowry theory, a base is defined as:
questions.exp: Under the Brønsted-Lowry definition, an acid is a substance that donates a proton (H<sup>+</sup>), while a base is a substance that accepts a proton.

questions.q: Which of the following solids is classified as a crystalline network solid rather than an amorphous solid?
questions.exp: Sodium chloride forms a highly structured crystalline lattice held together by strong ionic bonds, while glass, plastics, and rubber are amorphous solids.

questions.q: In an ionic bond, electrons are:
opts: Transferred from one atom to another
questions.exp: An ionic bond is formed through the electrostatic attraction between oppositely charged ions, which occurs when valence electrons are transferred from a metal to a non-metal.

questions.q: What will happen to the position of chemical equilibrium if hydroxide ions (OH<sup>-</sup>) are added to an aqueous solution of acetic acid (CH<sub>3</sub>COOH ⇔ CH<sub>3</sub>COO<sup>-</sup> + H<sup>+</sup>)?
opts: The equilibrium shifts to the left
The equilibrium shifts to the right
No change in equilibrium position occurs
questions.exp: Hydroxide ions react with hydronium ions (H<sup>+</sup>) to form water, removing H<sup>+</sup> from the solution. According to Le Chatelier's principle, the system will shift to the right to replenish the lost H<sup>+</sup> ions.

questions.q: Which of the following statements is true regarding the liquid state of matter?
opts: Liquids have a definite volume but take the shape of their container
Liquids have a definite shape and volume
Liquids have an indefinite volume but a definite shape
Liquids have neither a definite shape nor volume
questions.exp: Intermolecular forces in liquids are strong enough to hold particles in close proximity (definite volume), but not strong enough to lock them in fixed positions (indefinite shape).

questions.q:
Which of the following statements is true for the redox reaction:

Cr2O72- + 14H+ + 6Cl- → 2Cr3+ + 6Cl2 + 7H2O
questions.exp: The oxidation state of chlorine increases from -1 in Cl<sup>-</sup> to 0 in Cl<sub>2</sub>, which means chloride ions lose electrons and undergo oxidation.

questions.q: What is the conjugate base of carbonic acid (H<sub>2</sub>CO<sub>3</sub>)?
questions.exp: A conjugate base is formed when an acid donates a proton (H<sup>+</sup>). Removing one H<sup>+</sup> from H<sub>2</sub>CO<sub>3</sub> leaves the bicarbonate ion, HCO<sub>3</sub><sup>-</sup>.

questions.q: Which gas law relates the pressure and volume of a fixed mass of gas at constant temperature?
questions.exp: Boyle's Law states that the pressure of a given mass of an ideal gas is inversely proportional to its volume at constant temperature: P ∝ 1/V.

questions.q: Which of the following best describes the nature of metallic bonding?
opts: Electrons are shared equally between adjacent atoms
Electrons are transferred from one atom to another
Electrons are delocalized and free to move throughout the metallic lattice
Electrons are localized around each individual atom
questions.exp: Metallic bonding consists of a lattice of positive metal cations surrounded by a shared 'sea' of delocalized valence electrons that are free to move throughout the structure.

questions.q: Given a 0.1 M solution of a weak acid (K<sub>a</sub> = 1.8 × 10<sup>-5</sup>), what is the approximate pH of the solution?
questions.exp: For a weak acid: [H<sup>+</sup>] = √(K<sub>a</sub> × C) = √(1.8 × 10<sup>-5</sup> × 0.1) = √(1.8 × 10<sup>-6</sup>) ≈ 1.34 × 10<sup>-3</sup> M. Thus, pH = -log(1.34 × 10<sup>-3</sup>) ≈ 2.87.

questions.q: What happens to the kinetic energy of particles as a substance undergoes a phase transition from a solid to a liquid state?
opts: The kinetic energy remains completely constant
questions.exp: Heating a solid increases the kinetic energy of its particles, allowing them to break free from their fixed positions in the lattice to enter the liquid state.

questions.q: What type of intermolecular force is primarily responsible for the exceptionally high boiling point of water?
questions.exp: Water molecules can form highly stable intermolecular hydrogen bonds due to the large electronegativity difference between oxygen and hydrogen.

questions.q: Why does a buffer solution successfully resist significant changes in pH upon the addition of small amounts of an acid or a base?
opts: It contains only a strong mineral acid
It contains a weak acid and its conjugate base
It completely prevents the self-ionization of water
It physically absorbs all added ions
questions.exp: The weak acid component neutralizes added bases, while the conjugate base component neutralizes added acids, minimizing pH changes.

questions.q: What is the wavelength of the yellow sodium emission line, which has an electromagnetic frequency of 5.09 × 10<sup>14</sup> s<sup>-1</sup>? (Speed of light, C = 3 × 10<sup>8</sup> m/s)
questions.exp: Using λ = C / ν: λ = (3 × 10<sup>8</sup> m/s) / (5.09 × 10<sup>14</sup> s<sup>-1</sup>) ≈ 5.89 × 10<sup>-7</sup> m = 589 nm.

questions.q: In a covalent bond, how do non-metal atoms typically achieve a stable octet electron configuration?
opts: By transferring electrons from one atom to another
By sharing pairs of valence electrons
By losing all their core electrons
By gaining protons from other elements
questions.exp: Covalent bonds form when non-metal atoms share pairs of valence electrons to achieve stable, lower-energy octet configurations.

questions.q: Which of the following factors affects the ionization constant (K<sub>a</sub>) of a weak acid?
questions.exp: Equilibrium constants, including K<sub>a</sub>, are temperature-dependent. Changing the temperature changes the kinetic energy of the particles, shifting the position of the ionization equilibrium.

questions.q: In a diatomic molecule of nitrogen (N<sub>2</sub>), how many total valence electrons are shared between the two nitrogen atoms?
questions.exp: Nitrogen has 5 valence electrons and forms a triple bond (one σ and two π bonds) to complete its octet. A triple bond shares 3 pairs of electrons, which is 6 electrons.

questions.q: If the redox reaction Cu + HNO<sub>3</sub> → Cu(NO<sub>3</sub>)<sub>2</sub> + NO<sub>2</sub> + H<sub>2</sub>O is completely balanced, what is the stoichiometric coefficient of HNO<sub>3</sub>?
questions.exp: The balanced equation is: Cu + 4HNO<sub>3</sub> → Cu(NO<sub>3</sub>)<sub>2</sub> + 2NO<sub>2</sub> + 2H<sub>2</sub>O. The coefficient of HNO<sub>3</sub> is 4.

questions.q: A sample of gas occupies a volume of 10.0 L at 2.0 atm pressure. What will be its volume if the pressure is reduced to 1.0 atm at constant temperature?
questions.exp: Using Boyle's Law: P<sub>1</sub>V<sub>1</sub> = P<sub>2</sub>V<sub>2</sub> => 2.0 atm × 10.0 L = 1.0 atm × V<sub>2</sub> => V<sub>2</sub> = 20.0 L.

questions.q: A salt derived from a weak acid and a weak base will produce an aqueous solution whose pH depends directly on:
opts: The strength of the weak acid only
The strength of the weak base only
The relative strengths (K<sub>a</sub> vs K<sub>b</sub>) of the parent weak acid and weak base
The physical concentration of the salt alone
questions.exp: If K<sub>a</sub> > K<sub>b</sub>, the solution is acidic; if K<sub>b</sub> > K<sub>a</sub>, the solution is basic; if K<sub>a</sub> ≈ K<sub>b</sub>, the solution is neutral.

questions.q: At Standard Temperature and Pressure (STP), what volume will 2.0 moles of an ideal gas occupy?
questions.exp: One mole of an ideal gas occupies 22.4 L at STP. Therefore, 2.0 moles will occupy 2.0 × 22.4 L = 44.8 L.

questions.q: A student electrolyzes an aqueous solution of copper(II) sulfate using inert platinum electrodes. What will they observe at the anode?
questions.exp: In aqueous copper(II) sulfate, water is oxidized at the anode in preference to sulfate ions due to its lower oxidation potential: 2H<sub>2</sub>O → O<sub>2</sub>(g) + 4H<sup>+</sup> + 4e<sup>-</sup>. This releases oxygen gas.

questions.q: If a pH titration curve shows a single sharp pH rise around an equivalence point of pH 9, what type of acid-base titration is being performed?
questions.exp: Titrating a weak acid with a strong base yields a basic equivalence point (pH > 7) because the conjugate base of the weak acid hydrolyzes water to produce OH<sup>-</sup> ions.

questions.q: Which of the following represents the correct organizational parameter of elements in the Modern Periodic Table?
opts: Elements are arranged by increasing atomic number
Elements are arranged by increasing atomic mass
Elements are arranged by increasing alphabetical name
Elements are arranged by physical color
questions.exp: Moseley's law established that elements in the modern periodic table are arranged by increasing atomic number (number of protons in the nucleus).

questions.q: Which fundamental chemical law states that mass is neither created nor destroyed during a chemical reaction?
questions.exp: The Law of Conservation of Mass states that the total mass of the reactants before a chemical change is equal to the total mass of the products afterward.

questions.q: Which of the following is a key economic and technical characteristic of modern chemical industries?
opts: They avoid the use of chemical reactions to prevent contamination
They produce only organic monomer compounds
They consume large quantities of thermal and electrical energy
They do not perform product quality control testing
questions.exp: Chemical manufacturing is highly energy-intensive, requiring significant electrical and thermal energy to break and form chemical bonds.

questions.q: What is a major global environmental concern associated specifically with cement manufacturing?
questions.exp: Cement production releases large amounts of CO<sub>2</sub> both from the combustion of fossil fuels to heat the kiln and from the calcination of limestone (CaCO<sub>3</sub> → CaO + CO<sub>2</sub>).

questions.q: According to Dalton's atomic theory, atoms of the same element are:
opts: Different in size, mass, and properties
Identical in size, mass, and chemical properties
questions.exp: A core postulate of Dalton's original atomic theory was that all atoms of a given element are identical in mass and properties.

questions.q: What is the primary industrial raw material obtained directly from the Earth's atmosphere for use in chemical synthesis?
questions.exp: Atmospheric nitrogen (N<sub>2</sub>), which makes up about 78% of the air, is extracted by fractional distillation of liquid air and used to manufacture ammonia in the Haber-Bosch process.

questions.q: One of the most important advantages of the modern periodic classification of elements is that it helps in understanding:
opts: The periodicity of chemical and physical properties among elements
The exact cost of each element on the open market
The total number of atoms present in any compound
The physical appearance of all elements under standard conditions
questions.exp: The periodic table groups elements with similar electron configurations, making it easier to predict trends in reactivity, ionization energy, and electronegativity.

questions.q: Which of the following subatomic particles has a rest mass closest to that of a proton?
questions.exp: The mass of a proton is approximately 1.0073 amu, while the mass of a neutron is approximately 1.0087 amu. This makes the neutron the closest in mass.

questions.q: What is the primary societal and economic role played by industrial chemistry?
opts: It reduces overall technological development to conserve resources
It limits the scale of chemical product manufacturing
It prevents the use of natural resources
It supports economic growth by converting raw materials into useful products
questions.exp: Industrial chemistry transforms raw materials like air, water, and minerals into industrial, agricultural, and consumer products, supporting economic development.

questions.q: Which of the following groups of elements is correctly matched with its block in the periodic table?
questions.exp: Phosphorus (Z=15), Sulfur (Z=16), and Chlorine (Z=17) all have their outermost valence electrons in the p-subshell, making them p-block elements.

questions.q: What does the Aufbau principle state regarding the ground-state electron configuration of an atom?
opts: Electrons fill atomic orbitals in order of increasing energy
Electrons fill atomic orbitals in order of increasing atomic mass
Electrons fill atomic orbitals from higher to lower energy levels
Electrons populate available degenerate orbitals randomly
questions.exp: The Aufbau principle states that in the ground state of an atom or ion, electrons populate lower-energy subshells before moving to higher-energy ones.

questions.q: What is the primary industrial process used to manufacture ammonia on a commercial scale?
questions.exp: The Haber-Bosch process synthesizes ammonia (NH<sub>3</sub>) from nitrogen and hydrogen gas under high pressure and temperature using an iron catalyst.

questions.q: Which of the following natural energy resources is classified as renewable?
questions.exp: Solar energy is renewable because it is replenished naturally on a human timescale, unlike fossil fuels, which require millions of years to form.

questions.q: What is the value of the angular momentum (azimuthal) quantum number (l) for any d-orbital?
questions.exp: The azimuthal quantum number (l) determines orbital shape: l=0 is an s-orbital, l=1 is a p-orbital, l=2 is a d-orbital, and l=3 is an f-orbital.

questions.q: Which of the following energy resources is classified as non-renewable?
questions.exp: Fossil fuels (coal, oil, natural gas) are finite resources that cannot be replaced once depleted.

questions.q: How many unpaired electrons are present in the ground-state electron configuration of carbon (Z=6)?
questions.exp: The ground-state configuration of carbon is 1s<sup>2</sup> 2s<sup>2</sup> 2p<sup>2</sup>. According to Hund's rule, the two 2p electrons occupy separate degenerate p-orbitals with parallel spins, leaving 2 unpaired electrons.

questions.q: Which raw material is the primary source of silica used in glass manufacturing?
questions.exp: Sand (silicon dioxide, SiO<sub>2</sub>) is the main source of silica, which acts as the network-forming oxide in glass production.

questions.q: Which of the following forms of electromagnetic radiation has the shortest wavelength and highest energy?
questions.exp: On the electromagnetic spectrum, gamma rays have the highest frequency and shortest wavelength, giving them the highest energy per photon.

questions.q: Which local Ethiopian alcoholic beverage is traditionally manufactured on a commercial and household scale using distillation?
questions.exp: Araki is a traditional, home-distilled spirit made from fermented grains, distinct from non-distilled traditional beers like Tella.

questions.q: Which of the following catalysts is used in the Contact Process for the industrial manufacture of sulfuric acid?
questions.exp: Vanadium(V) oxide catalyzes the key step in the Contact Process: the oxidation of sulfur dioxide (SO<sub>2</sub>) to sulfur trioxide (SO<sub>3</sub>).

questions.q: Which of the following traditional methods is most commonly used to preserve food products in rural Ethiopia?
questions.exp: Sun drying is a low-cost, traditional method that removes moisture from foods like meat, grains, and red peppers to inhibit microbial growth.

questions.q: What is the primary industrial objective of the tanning process in leather production?
opts: To make the leather completely waterproof
To remove hair and epidermal tissue from hides
To bleach and color the leather fibers
To convert raw, putrescible hides into durable, stable leather
questions.exp: Tanning cross-links the collagen proteins in animal hides, preserving them and preventing rot to produce a durable, flexible material.

questions.q: Which byproduct of industrial sugar processing is used to manufacture bio-ethanol in Ethiopia?
questions.exp: Molasses, the thick syrup left after crystallizing sugar from cane juice, contains residual sugars that are fermented by yeast to produce ethanol.

questions.q: Which of the following minerals is added to Portland cement clinker during grinding to retard and control its setting time?
questions.exp: Without gypsum, cement reacts instantly with water and sets too quickly. Gypsum retards this hydration reaction, allowing workers time to mix and pour the concrete.

questions.q: What does the period number of an element indicate in the Modern Periodic Table?
opts: The principal energy level (shell) containing the valence electrons
The total number of protons in the atom's nucleus
The total number of valence electrons
The atomic weight of the element
questions.exp: The period number corresponds to the principal quantum number (n) of the outermost occupied electron shell in the ground-state atom.

questions.q: What is the primary product of industrial sugar manufacturing?
questions.exp: Refined sugar is primarily sucrose (C<sub>12</sub>H<sub>22</sub>O<sub>11</sub>), a disaccharide extracted from sugarcane or sugar beets.

questions.q: Which of the following is NOT a characteristic property of transition elements (d-block metals)?
opts: They are dense metals with high melting points
They are good conductors of heat and electricity
They form stable, brightly colored coordination compounds
They are highly reactive with cold water, releasing hydrogen gas
questions.exp: Unlike highly reactive s-block alkali metals, transition metals are relatively unreactive with water due to their high ionization energies and strong metallic bonding.

questions.q: Which type of glass is used to manufacture laboratory glassware due to its high thermal shock resistance and chemical durability?
questions.exp: Borosilicate glass contains boron trioxide, which gives it a very low coefficient of thermal expansion, preventing it from cracking under sudden temperature changes.

questions.q: Which of the following ions is preferentially discharged at the cathode during the electrolysis of aqueous copper(II) sulfate using inert platinum electrodes?
questions.exp: At the cathode, copper(II) ions have a higher reduction potential (+0.34 V) than hydrogen ions (0.00 V), so they are preferentially reduced to form copper metal.

questions.q: Which of the following crystal structures is hard, has a high melting point, and acts as an electrical insulator in both solid and liquid states?
questions.exp: Covalent network crystals like diamond or quartz consist of atoms held together by a continuous network of strong covalent bonds, with no free electrons or ions to conduct electricity.

questions.q: Why do different isotopes of the same element display nearly identical chemical properties?
opts: They have different nuclear energy levels
They have the same number of neutrons
They have the same number of protons and electrons
questions.exp: Chemical properties depend on an atom's electron configuration. Since isotopes of an element have the same number of protons, they have the same number of electrons in their neutral state.

questions.q: What is the first step in balancing a redox reaction using the half-reaction (ion-electron) method?
opts: Balance the oxygen atoms by adding water molecules
Separate the overall reaction into oxidation and reduction half-reactions
Balance the hydrogen atoms by adding hydrogen ions
Equalize the number of electrons transferred
questions.exp: To use the half-reaction method, you must first identify which species are oxidized and reduced and separate them into their respective half-reactions.

questions.q: Which of the following scientists is credited with discovering the dense, positively charged atomic nucleus using the gold foil scattering experiment?
questions.exp: Rutherford's 1911 gold foil experiment showed that alpha particles occasionally bounced backward, proving that most of an atom's mass and positive charge is concentrated in a tiny nucleus.

questions.q: Which of the following parameters determines which ions are preferentially discharged at an electrode during the electrolysis of an aqueous solution?
opts: The ambient temperature of the cell
The position of the ions in the electrochemical reactivity series and their concentrations
The voltage applied to the cell
The physical surface area of the electrodes
questions.exp: Ions with more favorable standard reduction/oxidation potentials are discharged first, though this can be influenced by high ion concentrations (overvoltage).

questions.q: According to the Bohr model of the atom, where are electrons located?
opts: Embedded uniformly inside a positively charged sphere
In fixed, circular orbits of quantized energy levels around the nucleus
In continuous, three-dimensional stationary energy clouds
questions.exp: Bohr proposed that electrons travel in defined circular orbits around the nucleus, with each orbit corresponding to a specific, quantized energy state.

questions.q: What is the primary consequence of removing the salt bridge from an operating galvanic (voltaic) cell?
opts: The chemical reaction continues completely unaffected
The electrical circuit is broken, charge builds up, and the cell stops working
The metallic electrodes begin to dissolve rapidly
questions.exp: The salt bridge maintains electrical neutrality by allowing ions to migrate between half-cells. Removing it breaks the circuit and stops the flow of electricity.

questions.q: Why do ionic compounds typically have exceptionally high melting and boiling points?
opts: They are stabilized by weak intermolecular forces
They are composed of overlapping covalent bonds
They have no structured molecular or lattice organization
The strong electrostatic forces in the crystal lattice require significant energy to break
questions.exp: Ionic solids form a closely packed three-dimensional lattice stabilized by strong, attractive electrostatic forces between cations and anions.

questions.q: Which of the following scientists proposed the first modern atomic theory supported by quantitative experimental mass relations?
questions.exp: John Dalton formulated his atomic theory in 1803, basing it on the laws of conservation of mass and definite proportions.

questions.q: Which of the following processes is NOT an industrial application of electrolysis?
opts: Extraction of reactive metals like aluminum
questions.exp: Nuclear fusion is a nuclear reaction that combines light atomic nuclei, which does not involve the electrolytic breakdown of chemical compounds.

questions.q: Which of the following molecules has a central atom that is sp<sup>3</sup> hybridized?
questions.exp: The central oxygen atom in water has two σ bonds and two lone pairs. This gives it a steric number of 4, which corresponds to sp<sup>3</sup> hybridization.

questions.q: Which model of the atom first proposed that electrons are embedded like raisin 'plums' inside a sphere of positive charge?
questions.exp: Following his discovery of the electron in 1897, J.J. Thomson proposed the 'plum pudding' model to account for the overall neutral charge of the atom.

questions.q: Which chemical bonding theory explains the formation of covalent bonds as the physical overlap of individual atomic orbitals?
questions.exp: Valence bond theory states that a covalent bond forms when half-filled valence atomic orbitals on two adjacent atoms overlap, pairing their spins.

questions.q: Which of the following molecules has a linear molecular geometry?
questions.exp: Carbon dioxide has two double bonds and no lone pairs on the central carbon atom, giving it a linear (180°) geometry.

questions.q: Which of the following is the correct cell notation for a galvanic cell composed of zinc and copper electrodes in their respective sulfate solutions?
questions.exp: By convention, the anode (oxidation) is written on the left and the cathode (reduction) on the right: Zn(s) → Zn<sup>2+</sup>(aq) + 2e<sup>-</sup> and Cu<sup>2+</sup>(aq) + 2e<sup>-</sup> → Cu(s).

questions.q: Which type of covalent bond is formed when two bonding atoms have identical electronegativities, resulting in equal sharing of the bonding electrons?
questions.exp: When the electronegativity difference (ΔEN) is close to zero, the bonding electrons are shared equally, forming a non-polar covalent bond.

questions.q: What is the hybridization of the central carbon atom in a molecule of carbon dioxide (CO<sub>2</sub>)?
questions.exp: The carbon atom forms two σ bonds and has no lone pairs (steric number of 2), which corresponds to sp hybridization.

questions.q: If a constant electric current of 5.0 A is passed through an electrolytic cell for exactly 2.0 hours, what is the total quantity of electrical charge passed?
questions.exp: Using Q = I × t: Q = 5.0 A × (2.0 hours × 3600 s/hour) = 5.0 A × 7200 s = 36,000 C.

questions.q: What is the ideal bond angle in a molecule with a regular tetrahedral molecular geometry, such as methane (CH<sub>4</sub>)?
questions.exp: In a symmetric tetrahedral molecule, the four bonding electron pairs are arranged to minimize repulsion, resulting in bond angles of 109.5°.

questions.q: Which branch of chemistry focuses specifically on separating, identifying, and determining the relative quantities of the components in a sample of matter?
questions.exp: Analytical chemistry is the study of the separation, identification, and quantification of the chemical components of natural and artificial materials.

questions.q: Which of the following represents a correct conversion factor between metric units of volume?
questions.exp: One liter (L) is defined as the volume of a cube with side lengths of 10 cm (1 dm). Therefore, 1 L is exactly equal to 1 cubic decimeter (dm<sup>3</sup>).

questions.q: What is considered the initial step in the systematic application of the scientific method?
questions.exp: The scientific method begins when an investigator makes an observation of a physical phenomenon, which raises a question that leads to a hypothesis.

questions.q: Which core postulate of Dalton's original atomic theory has been modified or disproved by modern atomic discoveries?
opts: Atoms combine in simple whole-number ratios to form chemical compounds
All matter is composed of small, indivisible particles called atoms
Atoms of different elements have different chemical properties
All atoms of the same element are completely identical in mass and properties
questions.exp: The discovery of isotopes proved that atoms of the same element can have different masses (due to different numbers of neutrons), disproving Dalton's postulate.

questions.q: Which region of the electromagnetic spectrum has the highest frequency and is capable of inducing electronic transitions of the greatest energy?
questions.exp: On the visible light spectrum, violet light has the shortest wavelength and highest frequency, carrying the highest energy per photon.

questions.q: What are the possible values of the azimuthal (orbital) quantum number (l) for an electron occupying an f-subshell?
questions.exp: The azimuthal quantum number (l) is fixed for each subshell type: l=0 (s), l=1 (p), l=2 (d), and l=3 (f).
